draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer conversational, approachable language over corporate formality
facade.label: Casual chat tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warm, relatable copy; use contractions; feel like talking to a colleague
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward polished, authoritative voice; formal grammar; expertise-first tone

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers warm, approachable interfaces over stark geometric precision
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into curved edges, softer shadows, and human-friendly aesthetics for the workspace
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer sharp, clean lines and minimal ornamentation—test crisp geometric design next

evidence.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer conversational, approachable language over corporate formality
evidence.implication: Lean into warm, relatable copy; use contractions; feel like talking to a colleague

facade.hypothesis: Users of an AI workspace prefer quantified metrics and KPIs over narrative summaries
facade.label: Dashboard with numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward real-time stat displays, numeric progress tracking, and metric-heavy dashboards
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward narrative-based progress summaries, status updates in prose form, and insight-driven text over raw numbers

facade.hypothesis: For an AI workspace, nesting secondary navigation (tabs) within a persistent sidebar reduces cognitive load by keeping related tools visible without horizontal scrolling.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation hierarchy — persistent vs. ephemeral, depth vs. flatness
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs inside
facade.acceptImplies: User values compact tool organization and visual consistency; likely prefers hierarchical structure and reduced jumping between contexts.
facade.rejectImplies: User favors flat, distributed navigation; may want tabs as primary scaffold or full-width content areas without persistent left column.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer snappy, zero-lag interactions without transition delays in an AI workspace context
facade.axisTargeted: Snappy vs Fluid — whether interactions should feel instantaneous (click → result) or smoothly animated (click → transition → result)
facade.label: Instant buttons
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace favors responsive, direct feedback: no motion overhead, crisp state changes, fast perceived performance over graceful transitions
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace benefits from animated flow: smooth state changes, motion to guide attention, transitions that feel intentional and guide the user through cognitive steps

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, considered transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, considered transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer high contrast between navigation and working area to reduce cognitive load
facade.label: Dark sidebar, bright canvas
facade.acceptImplies: Explore darker UI frames with luminous content zones; test sidebar-centric layouts
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward unified tonal schemes; test flat, even lighting across interface

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer professional, structured language over casual conversational style
facade.label: Formal tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into authoritative copy, industry terminology, and polished corporate language to signal competence and expertise
facade.rejectImplies: Casual conversational tone resonates more; users want approachable warmth even in a work tool, not corporate distance

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
